channel.title: # 🇱🇰 Sri Lanka This Week
channel.description: Generated by gpt-5 from 98 English News Articles published between 2026-04-08 and 2026-04-14.

item.title: Sri Lanka secures IMF staff-level deal for $700m tranche
item.description: Sri Lanka and the International Monetary Fund reached a staff-level agreement on the combined fifth and sixth reviews of the four-year Extended Fund Facility, paving the way for about $700 million, subject to IMF Executive Board approval. The IMF said the arrangement has so far disbursed $1.7 billion; Board approval of the new reviews would lift that to roughly $2.4 billion 💰.

item.title: Stocks rally as indices notch sharp gains on heavy turnover
item.description: The Colombo Stock Exchange recorded outsized moves during the week. On April 8 the All-Share Price Index rose 4.21% to 21,917.60 with turnover of Rs 6.61 billion, while on April 10 the ASPI added a further 284 points with Rs 5 billion changing hands; block trades included JKH, Commercial Bank and Chevron Lubricants.

item.title: Mazagon Dock cements control at Colombo Dockyard; new chair named
item.description: Colombo Dockyard PLC announced board changes effective April 7 after India’s Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd. took a 51% stake. Capt. (Retd.) Jagmohan was appointed non-executive Chairman, Managing Director/CEO Thimira S. Godakumbura remains on the board, and new non-executive directors include Vish Govindasamy and Senthilverl Nandhanan.

item.title: Northern line rail service resumes after India-backed repairs
item.description: Train services on the Northern Railway Line restarted following restoration of cyclone-damaged sections under a $5 million grant from India within a wider $450 million support package. The reopening ahead of the Sinhala and Tamil New Year restored a key inter-regional link 🚆.

item.title: Government launches Rs 2.16bn overhaul of Colombo Fort station
item.description: Authorities kicked off a 15‑month modernisation of Colombo Fort Railway Station under the Railway Efficiency Improvement Project, financed by the Asian Development Bank and the Government. Plans include automated ticketing, new lifts and pedestrian bridges, upgraded sanitation, and safety-focused building works to create a passenger-centric hub.

item.title: Commercial Bank and NCG Express roll out cashless bus ticketing
item.description: Commercial Bank of Ceylon partnered with private operator NCG Express to deploy digital ticketing that accepts contactless cards, QR and mobile wallets on board. Conductors are being equipped with devices linked to the bank’s acquiring network for instant settlement, creating a template for wider adoption in the private bus sector 💳.

item.title: Hayleys installs 800kW rooftop solar array for LSEG Sri Lanka
item.description: Hayleys Solar completed an 800 kW rooftop system across six buildings for the London Stock Exchange Group’s Sri Lanka operations within two months. The 1,412-panel installation is expected to produce about 1,250 MWh a year, meeting close to 80% of daytime demand and cutting roughly 850 tonnes of CO₂ annually ⚡.

item.title: Vidullanka buys Gurugoda mini hydro operator, adding 4.5 MW
item.description: Vidullanka PLC acquired 100% of Bhoruka Power Lanka Ltd., which owns and runs the 4.5 MW Gurugoda mini hydro plant in Aranayake. The deal expands Vidullanka’s domestic mini-hydro fleet to 12 projects and lifts its total project portfolio to 26, alongside recent solar, wind and storage wins.

item.title: Control Union Sri Lanka issues world’s first DFS group certificate
item.description: Control Union Sri Lanka awarded the first-ever Deforestation‑Free Standard (DFS) Group Certificate to Samson Rubber Industries, following its earlier issuance of the world’s first single-entity DFS certificate to Hapugastenne Plantations PLC. The standard is designed to help producers and supply chains align with the EU Deforestation Regulation.

item.title: Motor Traffic chief remanded to April 20 in registration probe
item.description: The Pilessa Magistrate’s Court in Kurunegala ordered further remand of Motor Traffic Department Commissioner General Kamal Amarasinghe until April 20. Police said he was arrested on March 27 and charged with aiding and abetting the fraudulent registration of a vehicle by altering the chassis number.
